steps.action: Attan (Pashto: اتڼ [aˈt̪aɳ] ) is a Pashtun dance with weight transfer through footwork, pivot turn, hold hands, partner opposite.
steps.text: Attan (Pashto: اتڼ [aˈt̪aɳ] ) is a Pashtun dance with weight transfer through footwork, pivot turn, hold hands, partner opposite.

steps.action: It is performed during weddings or other celebra
steps.text: It is performed during weddings or other celebrations (engagements, weddings and informal gatherings).

steps.action: It is now considered the national dance of Afgha
steps.text: It is now considered the national dance of Afghanistan, popularly carried by other ethnic groups in Afghanistan as well as by the Pashtuns of Pakistan.

steps.action: Attan is usually performed with a Dohol, which i
steps.text: Attan is usually performed with a Dohol, which is a double-headed barrel drum.

steps.action: The dance can be anywhere from 5 to 30 minutes l
steps.text: The dance can be anywhere from 5 to 30 minutes long.

steps.action: Attan is performed traditionally segregated alth
steps.text: Attan is performed traditionally segregated although in modern times, Afghans have performed the dance with both men and women.

steps.action: Closely related circle dances can be found in th
steps.text: Closely related circle dances can be found in the Khorasan and Sistan and Baluchestan provinces of Iran, commonly known as Chapi or Torbati there ("رقص خراسانی"or "رقص سیستانی").

steps.action: The dance dates back nearly 3,000 years and was
steps.text: The dance dates back nearly 3,000 years and was performed during Zoroastrian religious ceremonies.

steps.action: According to Zoroastrian folklore, King Yama, a
steps.text: According to Zoroastrian folklore, King Yama, a figure in Iranic mythology, celebrated Nawroz by performing an Attan with his warriors.

steps.action: During King Yama's time, Attan was performed bef
steps.text: During King Yama's time, Attan was performed before going to war because it used to give the army the confidence that they could win the battle.

steps.action: As well as being the national dance of Afghanist
steps.text: As well as being the national dance of Afghanistan, attan is also a very popular part of festivals, weddings, and other forms of celebrations.

steps.action: Some believe the dance is connected to Ancient G
steps.text: Some believe the dance is connected to Ancient Greece and the time of Alexander, connecting the Attan to the ancient Pyrrhic war dance.

steps.action: The dance starts with slow steps that gradually
steps.text: The dance starts with slow steps that gradually get faster and faster until it seems the performers must drop from exhaustion.

steps.action: However, the dance continues, sometimes for two
steps.text: However, the dance continues, sometimes for two or three hours at a stretch, with no breaks except a lowering of tempo or changes in the tunes and songs.

steps.action: What the Attan Dance consists of: The dancers ga
steps.text: What the Attan Dance consists of: The dancers gather in a circle, and then is followed by music which starts slow at first, and then gradually speeds up.

steps.action: There is a consistent beat and rhythm, and durin
steps.text: There is a consistent beat and rhythm, and during that specific beat is when they clap inside the circle, so the movement of the hands is outside prior to the beat.

steps.action: It is then followed by the dancers bringing thei
steps.text: It is then followed by the dancers bringing their hands out and then clapping inside the circle, and it is the same routine and pattern of movement, which then get faster.

steps.action: As the movements and routine get faster, the one
steps.text: As the movements and routine get faster, the one clap turns into two claps, and the dancers who are more advanced, at times will add turns into the movements.

steps.action: All in all, they must keep the circular path wit
steps.text: All in all, they must keep the circular path with the clap on the beat, every other move added is up to the individuals who originate and add their own personal style to the dance.

steps.action: Common dance moves involve the extending of arms
steps.text: Common dance moves involve the extending of arms into air and the stretch and extension of legs.

steps.action: The tiny mirrors add great detail and shine unde
steps.text: The tiny mirrors add great detail and shine under the lights as the women move and dance.

steps.action: Men would also wear traditional turbans referred
steps.text: Men would also wear traditional turbans referred to as Lungee or Patkya which signifies what tribes they’re from and holds great importance.

steps.action: All different kinds of Attan are danced with the
steps.text: All different kinds of Attan are danced with the beats of the drums.

steps.action: The beater of the drum known as "Dum", who insta
steps.text: The beater of the drum known as "Dum", who instantaneously change the rhythm, is circled by the performers.
